project.title: Identification of lactoylation on PHGDH
project.projectDescription: Sites of lactoylation were identified on both recominant human PHGDH and PHGDH in cellulo. For recombinant protein, sample was treated with 100 uM LGSH for 24 hours. For in cellulo, cells were transfected with PHGDH-mycDDK and treated with 50 uM MGO for lactoylLys identification.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: From the paper methods:  For in cellulo: Lysates (100 µg) were incubated end-over-end with DYKDDDDK affinity resin (50 µL slurry) overnight at 4°C. The following day, the resin was washed 5X with PBS and resuspended in 25 µL of 4X loading buffer. Samples were boiled at 95°C for 10 minutes and diluted in 75 µL ddH2O. Protein was resolved via SDS-PAGE as described above. For proteomics, gel bands were excised as described below. LC-MS/MS analysis was done on a QExactive Plus mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) equipped with an EASY-Spray nanoESI source. Peptides were eluted from an Acclaim Pepmap 100 trap column (75 micron ID x 2 cm, Thermo Scientific) onto an Acclaim PepMap RSLC analytical column (75 micron ID × 25 cm, Thermo Scientific using a 3–25% gradient of solvent B (ACN, 0.1% formic acid) over 90 min, 25–50% solvent B over 20 min, 50–95% of solvent B over 5 min, then a hold of solvent 95% B for 10 min, and finally a return to 3% solvent B for 10 min. Solvent A consisted of water and 0.1% formic acid. Flow rates were 300 nL min-1 using a Dionex Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano System (Thermo Scientific). Data dependent scanning was performed by the Xcalibur v 4.0.27.19 software[28] using a survey scan at 70,000 resolution scanning mass/charge (m/z) 350–1600 at an automatic gain control (AGC) target of 1e6 and a maximum injection time (IT) of 65 msec, followed by higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) at 27nce (normalized collision energy), of the 11 most intense ions at a resolution of 17,500, an isolation width of 1.5 m/z, an AGC of 5e4 and a maximum IT of 65 msec. Dynamic exclusion was set to place any selected m/z on an exclusion list for 30 seconds after a single MS/MS. Ions of charge state +1, 7, 8, >8, unassigned, and isotopes were excluded from MS/MS.  For recombinant: Recombinant human His-tagged PHGDH (1 µg) was incubated with LGSH (vehicle, 100 µM, 500 µM, 1 mM, 5 mM) for 24 h at 37°C in a final volume of 20 µL. Protein was denatured, boiled (95°C, 10 minutes), resolved using SDS-PAGE and stained with Coomassie. Following destaining with water, gel bands were excised and destained with 200 µL of 50% ACN in 25 mM ammonium bicarbonate while rotating end-over-end at room temperature. Solvent was removed and gel bands were fully dehydrated in ACN. Solvent was removed and 50 µL of 45 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) prepared in 25 mM ammonium bicarbonate was added and incubated for 30 minutes at 37°C. After incubation, 50 µL of 100 mM iodoacetamide (IAA) prepared in 25 mM ammonium bicarbonate was added and incubated for 45 minutes in the dark. All solvent was removed, and bands were rinsed 3X with 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate. Bands were dehydrated again with ACN. Bands were then rehydrated in 25 mM ammonium bicarbonate containing 10 ng µL-1 trypsin and incubated for 16 h at 37°C. After incubation, solvent was removed and placed in new Eppendorf tube. Bands were dehydrated in ACN and solvent was pooled with previous step. Samples were dried down under N2 and analyzed.  LC-MS/MS analysis was done on a Q Exactive Plus mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) equipped with an EASY-Spray nanoESI source. Peptides were eluted from an Acclaim Pepmap 100 trap column (75 micron ID x 2 cm, Thermo Scientific) onto an Acclaim PepMap RSLC analytical column (75 micron ID × 25 cm, Thermo Scientific using a 3–20% gradient of solvent B (ACN, 0.1% formic acid) over 40 min, 20–50% solvent B over 5 min, 50–95% of solvent B over 5 min, then a hold of solvent 95% B for 10 min, and finally a return to 3% solvent B for 10 min. Solvent A consisted of water and 0.1% formic acid. Flow rates were 300 nL min-1 using a Dionex Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano System (Thermo Scientific). Data dependent scanning was performed by the Xcalibur v 4.0.27.19 software[28] using a survey scan at 70,000 resolution scanning mass/charge (m/z) 350–1600 at an automatic gain control (AGC) target of 1e6 and a maximum injection time (IT) of 65 msec, followed by higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) at 27nce (normalized collision energy), of the 11 most intense ions at a resolution of 17,500, an isolation width of 1.5 m/z, an AGC of 5e4 and a maximum IT of 65 msec. Dynamic exclusion was set to place any selected m/z on an exclusion list for 30 seconds after a single MS/MS. Ions of charge state +1, 7, 8, >8, unassigned, and isotopes were excluded from MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Tandem mass spectra were searched against homo sapiens protein database from SwissProt (Nov 5 2018, 42252 sequences). All MS/MS spectra were searched using Thermo Proteome Discoverer v 2.2.0388 (ThermoFisher Scientific) considering fully tryptic peptides with up to 2 missed cleavage sites. Variable modifications considered during the search included methionine oxidation (15.995 Da), cysteine carbamidomethylation (57.021 Da), +lactoylLys (+72.012 Da). Proteins were identified at 99% confidence with XCorr score cut-offs as determined by a reversed database search[29]. Protein and peptide identification results were also visualized with Scaffold Q+S v 4.8.7 (Proteome Software Inc., Portland OR), a program that relies on various search engine results (i.e.: Sequest, X!Tandem, MASCOT) and which uses Bayesian statistics to reliably identify more spectra[30].
